description_long: This is a roughly rectangular stone block with a pale, off-white coloration and a visibly weathered surface. One face of the block features intricate, interwoven knotwork carvings, reminiscent of Celtic or medieval decorative motifs, while another face displays more eroded, indistinct relief patterns. The remaining sides are mostly rough and unadorned, showing natural stone texture and some chipping along the edges. The block appears solid and heavy, with a dense, granular stone composition. Its surface is matte and uneven, with clear evidence of age and exposure to the elements. The overall appearance suggests it may have served as a decorative or structural element in a historical or heritage urban setting.
description: A heavy, pale stone block with intricate knotwork carvings on one face and rough, weathered surfaces on the others. The block shows signs of age and was likely used decoratively or structurally in a historical context.
description_view_0: This view shows a narrow side with visible knotwork carvings and a rough, chipped top edge.
description_view_1: This view highlights a broader face with eroded, indistinct relief patterns and a generally weathered appearance.
description_view_2: This view displays a plain, rough side with minimal decoration and natural stone texture.
description_view_3: This view presents the most detailed knotwork carvings, covering a large portion of the block's face.
attribute_location_context: historic urban park or plaza with decorative stonework
attribute_installation_method: dry-stacked or set on a prepared stone or earth base
attribute_weathering_pattern: Surface shows signs of erosion and chipping, with softened edges and some discoloration from age.
attribute_presence_of_markings_or_engrav: Visible knotwork carvings and eroded relief patterns on two faces.
attribute_integration_with_surrounding_s: Likely interlocked or aligned with other decorative or structural stone blocks.
